rationale: Both correctly diagnose the None+1 crash from missing extended ratio tokens and give the same confirmed workaround, but X explicitly cites PR #2713 and the old `modes`-filtering regression that akshatvishu identified as the actual root cause of why the config forces the wrong topology — Y omits this entirely and instead treats the two-stage config as inherently normal. Y also speculatively proposes a specific code fix (empty-list degrade, safe range on line 1565) without verifying it against the actual downstream code, which is riskier/less grounded than X's more restrained proper-fix suggestion. Both correctly land on deferring the guard/docs fix to a new tracking issue per Gaohan123.
